title: Conflict Resolution: Staging Merge Verification for helix-cli
description: This tour walks through a conflict-resolution run triggered by a staging refresh merge into the BLD-448 feature branch. The sole conflicted file was src/tickets/index.ts (ticket subcommand dispatcher), which the auto-merge resolved cleanly with zero markers remaining. No source code changes were required. The tour verifies that all 10 ticket subcommands are intact, the library CLI module (src/library/) is unaffected, the main dispatcher (src/index.ts) still registers the library module, and SKILL.md documentation is complete — all confirmed by TypeScript build passing with zero errors.

steps.description: The staging refresh merged one staging commit (6a4215c) into the BLD-448 branch which carried four ticket-side commits (5cfbd79, ca6c51b, 210d9fc, 758fce3). Both sides had modified src/tickets/index.ts — the ticket subcommand dispatcher — causing a recorded conflict. Git's auto-merge resolved it cleanly. This diagnosis document confirms zero conflict markers remain and that the library CLI module (src/library/) was entirely on the ticket side and thus never involved in any conflict.
steps.title: Overview: What Was Merged and Why

steps.description: The first diagnostic question asked whether src/tickets/index.ts still contained active conflict markers. Evidence: grep for '<<<<<<<' returned zero matches. The file is 150 lines and contains a switch statement dispatching all 10 expected ticket subcommands (list, latest, get, create, update-description, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle) with all corresponding imports present. The auto-merge preserved both ticket-side and staging-side intents.
steps.title: Diagnosis Q1: Conflict Markers in tickets/index.ts

steps.description: The second diagnostic question confirmed the library module is entirely separate from the conflicted file. merge-conflicts.json lists ONLY src/tickets/index.ts — no library file appears. The scout reference-map confirms all 9 Phase 2b steps are complete: 6 library command files, the resolver utility, the dispatcher registration in src/index.ts, and SKILL.md. Data flow for library commands enters through src/index.ts and routes to src/library/index.ts, bypassing the ticket dispatcher entirely.
steps.title: Diagnosis Q2: Library Module Isolation Confirmed

steps.description: Because the auto-merge resolved cleanly, the implementation plan specifies zero source code edits. The three steps are: (1) verify zero conflict markers repo-wide via grep, (2) install dependencies so node_modules is current, and (3) run 'npm run build' (which invokes tsc) as the primary correctness gate. A clean TypeScript compile proves both ticket-side and staging-side changes coexist without type or import errors introduced by the merge.
steps.title: Implementation Plan: No Source Changes Required

steps.description: The implementation-plan APL records that src/tickets/index.ts contains all 10 imports (list, latest, get, create, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle, update-description) and a matching switch statement with 10 cases plus a default handler. This is the data-flow entry point for every 'helix ticket <subcommand>' invocation. Confirming all 10 cases are present ensures no ticket functionality was lost during the merge.
steps.title: Implementation Plan APL: All 10 Subcommands Verified

steps.description: The implementation APL records three confirmed outcomes: (1) zero conflict markers in src/tickets/index.ts and across all src/ files, (2) tsc exited with code 0 and zero errors (triggered via npm install prepare script), and (3) all 10 ticket subcommand imports are at lines 4-13 of tickets/index.ts and the library module is registered in src/index.ts at lines 14, 54-57, and 98. A passing TypeScript build is the strongest signal that the merge did not break any import or type contract.
steps.title: Implementation Results: Build Passed, Zero Markers

steps.description: The code review walked all 8 relevant files and marked each OK. The library data flow is: src/index.ts (main entry, line 98-102 library case) → src/library/index.ts (routes list/show/comments) → src/library/list.ts, show.ts, comments.ts → src/library/comments-list.ts and comments-post.ts. Each file's purpose and verdict is recorded in the review table. No issues were found; all four library commands (library list, library show, library comments list, library comments post) are correctly implemented.
steps.title: Code Review: All Library Files Verified Intact

steps.description: The code-review APL answers three targeted questions: (1) all 6 library source files are present and functional, (2) src/index.ts registers the library module at the import (line 14), help text (lines 54-57), and case handler (line 98-102), and (3) SKILL.md documents all 4 library commands with usage examples, rating values, and --reply-to support in both the commands table (lines 48-51) and workflow section (lines 146-179). The final APL statement — 'No code changes required' — closes the conflict-resolution run.
steps.title: Code Review APL: Documentation and Registration Confirmed

steps.description: The conflict-resolution run is complete with zero source changes. The major code paths are all verified clean: the ticket dispatcher (src/tickets/index.ts) has all 10 subcommand routes, the library module (src/library/) has all 6 command files registered through src/index.ts, SKILL.md is fully documented, and tsc reports zero errors. The codebase is ready to continue feature development on the BLD-448 branch.
steps.title: Summary: Conflict Resolution Complete, All Code Paths Clean
